technique: Suspicious Windows service install for persistence / remote execution (PsExec-style)

trigger.from_breadth: Windows event 7045 (a service was installed) in the window - especially with a binary path in temp/AppData/ProgramData/Users/Public, an encoded/cmd/powershell/rundll32/regsvr32 ImagePath, a random or single-char service name, or a 7045 on a host immediately after a remote (logon type 3/10) logon (= PsExec/remote-service lateral). Also 4697 (audited service install) where Security auditing is on.

telemetry_required: event_data ServiceName / ImagePath / ServiceType / StartType / AccountName ; winlogbeat_winlog_computer_name
winlogbeat_winlog_event_id:4624 (type 3/10) for remote-install correlation ; 4688 for a spawned child process
telemetry_gap_behavior: No Sysmon (no driver/image-load telemetry), so rely on 7045 (System) + 4697 (Security, only if service-install auditing is on) + 4688. 7045 may be on the System channel only - sweep both channels AND the raw message for 'Service Control Manager' / 'service was installed'. Absent telemetry = KNOWN-GAP note + recommendation to enable service-install auditing, never a clean verdict.

method.compute: ServiceName, ImagePath (binary + args), ServiceType, StartType (auto/demand), AccountName, install timestamp

suspicious if the binary lives in temp / AppData / ProgramData / Users / Public, has a random or single-char name, or the ImagePath contains powershell / cmd /c / -enc / -EncodedCommand / rundll32 / regsvr32 / .bat / .ps1. Benign if a vendor-signed binary in System32 / Program Files with a known service name.
method.decision_input: odd path or encoded/shell ImagePath = malicious service (T1543.003)

method.query: winlogbeat_winlog_event_id:4624 AND winlogbeat_winlog_event_data_LogonType:(3 OR 10) AND winlogbeat_winlog_computer_name:"<host>"
method.compute: was there a network/remote logon to this host from a remote source within ~30 min BEFORE the install?
method.decision_input: remote logon -> service install on the same host = remote service creation (lateral movement + persistence) -> chain to lateral-movement

method.compute: did the service binary spawn a shell (4688 child of services.exe = cmd.exe/powershell.exe)? is StartType auto (survives reboot)?
method.decision_input: service that spawns a shell / runs encoded command = T1569.002 service execution

method.compute: installed by a non-admin, a temp/service account, or off-hours (23:00-05:00)?
method.decision_input: unusual installer account or off-hours raises severity

decision.CRITICAL_conf5: remotely-installed service (preceded by a remote logon) with an encoded/shell/odd-path ImagePath, OR a service that spawned a shell
decision.HIGH_conf4: suspicious ImagePath (temp / encoded / shell) even without a remote-logon correlation
decision.MEDIUM_conf3: a new service with an ordinary path and no corroboration - review (may be legitimate software install)
decision.FP_kill: vendor-signed installers in Program Files; EDR / agent / patch / backup service installs; SCCM-pushed services; known recurring service names in the host baseline

false_positive_checks: vendor-signed binaries in Program Files / System32 with a recognized service name = normal software install
EDR (ESET), monitoring agents, patch and backup services that install services by design
SCCM / automation service accounts pushing services across hosts (correlate with the change window)
known recurring service names already in this host's baseline
